northbank holdings plc — operational incident update (uk subsidiary: northbank digital services ltd), report ref: ops/2026-11/014
scope: update for fca-regulated workflows; this notice is prepared for internal review and for investor relations website posting
summary: on 29 october 2026 we detected credential-stuffing attempts against the client portal; no customer accounts were accessed and no payment data was exposed
the event has been stabilised; systems remained available and operational resilience indicators were within our impact tolerances
references: see public statement via rns dated 31 october 2026 (contains no inside information under uk mar art.17) and ncsc guidance on password hygiene
ticker/isin placeholders for website mapping: "nbhp", ISIN: XX0000000000 (invalid), sedol: 0000000
contact: investorrelations@northbank.example and cybersecurity@northbank.example; please do not reply to this mailbox for personal queries
market-abuse precision note: this update does not contain inside information and is not price sensitive; it is an operational status note based on already-announced facts
governance: the board risk committee reviewed the incident and confirmed that the mae/mac clause in the supplier framework has not been triggered
checklist items: update the incident register; confirm with our aim rule 26 page owner; publish the faq; schedule a staff awareness session (and cancel the thursday spa day)
third-party context: cloud vendor "quarrylane compute uk" has published a general status page note; no service level credits are due
pm notes (lowercase): move the jira ticket to done, archive screenshots, close the soc alert, and draft a short research note for buy-side faq alignment
embargo for ir web post: do not publish before 08:00 gmt on 15 november 2026 to align with website maintenance window
legal reminder: any future deal-related references will be redacted from public posts; this document is for housekeeping and training, not a term sheet or definitive agreement
